channel.title: Daily Feed (Last 48 Hours, Updated 9 AM BD)
channel.description: Daily Feed (Last 48 Hours, Updated 9 AM BD) generated by script

item.title: U.S. Military Imposes Blockade on Iranian Ports in Strait of Hormuz
item.description: But without international backing, the blockade risks upending Washington’s already fragile cease-fire with Tehran.

item.title: Tehran Can’t Count on Hormuz
item.description: The regime survived, but this doesn’t make it as strong as it thinks.

item.title: From Ralph Lauren to The Row, American luxury is booming
item.description: Can the old world fight back?

item.title: How Hungary can now lead the fight against illiberalism
item.description: Having vanquished Viktor Orban, Peter Magyar must get to work

item.title: The War Room newsletter: The consequences of the Hormuz blockade
item.description: Shashank Joshi, our defence editor, analyses what blocking off the strait would mean for oil prices, geopolitics and Donald Trump’s political calendar

item.title: In the Gulf, Ukraine flaunts its skill at intercepting drones
item.description: Deals with Saudi Arabia, Qatar and the United Arab Emirates show it is now a defence-industry power

item.title: Spanish Immigration Is Different
item.description: Madrid is preparing for one of the most ambitious regularization schemes in European history.

item.title: How to Prevent 9 Million Deaths
item.description: Rich countries must understand foreign aid can actually be popular—if people see the results.

item.title: Why the Trump administration is waging war on fraud
item.description: Medicare and Medicaid have problems. But that is not the whole story

item.title: Donald Trump’s blockade of Hormuz is a dangerous gamble
item.description: It further undermines international law and could lead to new fighting

item.title: Will the IMF Ever Learn?
item.description: From austerity to taxation, the International Monetary Fund has consistently failed to incorporate its own findings into its lending programs. The Fund's once-a-decade Review of Program Design and Conditionality, which is now underway, offers a critical opportunity to change this.

item.title: Iran War Tests BRICS — And Reveals its Limits
item.description: Despite calls for mediation, the grouping has remained fragmented, highlighting its role as a “club” like the G-7 rather than a forum for collective action.

item.title: To Work for Us, AI Must Not Think for Us
item.description: The public discussion about AI’s impact on society focuses largely on the potential displacement of workers and the loss of jobs. But an even greater risk is the displacement of human thought and the processes that produce the knowledge base on which AI models themselves rely.

item.title: China’s pension failings expose its harshest inequality
item.description: Uneven hand-outs see the rural elderly till the soil while urbanites enjoy retirement

item.title: The World Is Learning to Work Around America
item.description: The Iran ceasefire has exposed the limits of American power, as the United States was forced to rely on others to contain a crisis of its own making. It offers a glimpse of a possible future in which the countries of the Global South have both the will and the means to navigate crises on their own terms.

item.title: India on the High Seas: Hormuz and the Future of Maritime Security
item.description: Vice Admiral R.B. Pandit, former Commander-in-Chief of India's Strategic Forces Command, assesses the maritime consequences of the Iran conflict and the long-term shifts reshaping the Indian Ocean's strategic order.

item.title: Japan Is America’s Indispensable Ally
item.description: Trump, China, and the Iran War make this point even clearer.

item.title: Pressed by Russian Drones, Ukraine Turns to Ground Robots
item.description: While mostly used for logistics, Ukraine is increasingly using them for combat operations.

item.title: What Hungary Can Learn From Poland
item.description: Following Tisza’s victory over Viktor Orbán’s ruling Fidesz party in Sunday’s election, the opposition faces an uphill battle in trying to restore Hungary’s democracy. Tisza leader Péter Magyar has much to learn from Polish Prime Minister Donald Tusk, especially the importance of experimenting with bold solutions.

item.title: The Domestic Politics of Cheng Li-wun’s China Trip
item.description: Cheng's meeting with Xi Jinping was not just about cross-strait dynamics, but about securing her own position within the KMT.

item.title: The tech jobs bust is real. Don’t blame AI (yet)
item.description: Why technology firms are shedding workers

item.title: Dependence Without Trust: The Drivers of Pakistan-UAE Divergence
item.description: The recent geopolitical turbulence surrounding the United States-Israel-Iran conflict may become a pivotal moment for Pakistan-United Arab Emirates ties after nearly a decade of simmering mistrust.

item.title: With Orban Gone, China Has Lost Its Best Friend in the EU
item.description: During Viktor Orban’s 16 years as the prime minister, Hungary became the most China-friendly EU country. Now Beijing may encounter a more united Europe.

item.title: Tokayev, Iran, and the Erosion of Kazakhstan’s Multi-Vector Diplomacy
item.description: When a state consistently speaks of independence and equidistance, yet acts in a clearly asymmetrical manner in critical moments, geopolitical partners take note.

item.title: The Investment Implications of Japan’s Cross-Border Carbon Trade
item.description: One of Asia's most strategically significant decarbonization investment opportunities

item.title: Which Asian Countries Are Most at Risk From the Iran Energy Shock?
item.description: Across Asia, factories are closing, flagship airlines are cutting routes, and people are lining up for hours to buy cooking gas, all because of the war in Iran.

item.title: After Orbán, Hungary Faces an Even Harder Battle
item.description: Center-right Tisza’s victory in Hungary’s election shows that even a highly entrenched new-right regime can be defeated at the polls. But the myths, resentments, and paranoia that fueled popular support for Viktor Orbán’s brand of illiberalism have not suddenly vanished with his defeat.

item.title: How the Pentagon Can Manage the Risks of AI Warfare
item.description: If warfighters don’t trust the technology, they won’t use it.

item.title: Why the US-Iran Talks in Pakistan Ended in a Deadlock
item.description: The expectation that the adversaries would reach a quick deal after weeks of a destructive war was always unrealistic.

item.title: Why Orbán Lost
item.description: The downfall of Hungary's autocratic prime minister, whose model of "illiberal democracy"  became a lodestar to many on the right—was a consequence of that model's own logic. Whatever happens next, one thing is certain: democracy's friends and foes alike will be watching closely.

item.title: Philippines Says Any Joint Oil Exploration With China Will Follow Country’s Constitution
item.description: Last month, President Ferdinand Marcos Jr. announced that he was open to resumption of joint oil exploration in the disputed South China Sea.

item.title: Amb. Stephen Rapp on How the Ground Is Being Prepared for Ethnic Cleansing in India
item.description: “The non-stop incitement and conduct of anti-Muslim violence, in the absence of any accountability for perpetrators, has resulted in the normalization of persecution.”

item.title: Why the India-Bangladesh Ganges Treaty Renewal Must Deliver Real Security
item.description: The treaty will expire later this year. But it was not discussed during Bangladesh Foreign Minister Khalilur Rahman’s recent visit to Delhi.

item.title: Three Indian Nationals Killed in Myanmar Civil War
item.description: The three men were captured by the rebel People’s Defense Army but subsequently tortured and killed by a rival outfit.

item.title: How to End the Iran Crisis
item.description: Tehran needs positive incentives, not just pressure.

item.title: The Trouble With Permanent Alliances
item.description: America’s pacts should have expiration dates.

item.title: Uncertainty Looms as US Sanctions Waiver for India Expires
item.description: The geopolitical uncertainties in the Gulf have created new opportunities for India and Russia to strengthen ties.

item.title: Indonesia’s Disinformation Bill and the Politics of ‘Foreign Propaganda’
item.description: In its bid to clamp down on fake news, President Prabowo's administration is arming itself with some concerning powers.

item.title: Australian PM to Travel to Brunei and Malaysia to Secure Energy, Fertilizer Supplies
item.description: The trip comes days after the Australian leader brokered a deal with Singapore to ensure a continued flow of fuel and gas between the two nations.

item.title: Peter Magyar topples illiberal hero Viktor Orban in Hungary
item.description: The upstart hands the populist a crushing defeat
